what_it_does: Implements the memory-retrieval seam for the Live Decision Surface: given a decision's text and optional address, it queries the shared vector corpus across configured spaces, optionally reranks with jina-v3, attaches legible digest text, resolves decision-specific provenance, and assembles a context bundle (recall_for_decision) plus a richer explanation bundle (explanation_grounding) that includes prior decisions, neighbours, theorem-fork framework claims, and a single operator-legible block for the RHM.
observations: Defines DEFAULT_DECISION_SPACES and EXPLAIN_DECISION_SPACES; the hot path deliberately omits the large 'extractions' space to stay under a 3s budget.
Uses a live-calibrated rerank relevance floor _CTX_FLOOR = -0.13 to reject off-domain noise while admitting weak real signal.
Caches theorem-fork claim sets in _THEOREM_CLAIMS_CACHE keyed by (decision_text, vector_version) to avoid repeated expensive determine/cluster steps.
Provides server-side element texts (caveat and grounding_source) for theorem-fork decisions, surfaced both in-card and in explanation.
recall_for_decision embeds the query once and snapshots vector records once, then queries each space against the shared embedding and snapshot to avoid Nx redundant work.
explanation_grounding skips the full corpus recall for theorem-fork and for rerank=False, using decision content + provenance or theorem claims instead.
Theorem-fork explanations are framework-only: grounded in Tim's written mathematics with a mandatory AI-projection caveat and no surrounding corpus context.
_provenance_text resolves board:// and code:// explanation_source values, including section-scoped anchors for multi-item documents.
_attach_digest_text fetches corpus records in one pass and attaches clean meaning text; extraction:// items are resolved via read_extraction.
prior_decisions_about searches history and common_knowledge for decision-shaped content about a topic.
Heavy inline design-history comments include dates, measured timings, regression names, and calibration rationales.
standouts: Structural never-assert law for theorem-fork decisions: framework-only grounding plus explicit AI-projection caveat.
Single shared embedding and vector snapshot across multiple spaces to meet the 3s card-resolve budget.
Live-calibrated jina-v3 rerank floor (-0.13) with documented measurement gap.
Authoritative explain-turn grounding (explanation_grounding) kept separate from the fast card-resolve path.
conventions: Reuses existing modules (suite.query_corpus, corpus_rerank, corpus_neighbours, recall_determine) rather than duplicating memory stores.
Degrade-clean error handling: catches exceptions, records notes, and returns partial bundles instead of raising.
Operator-legibility: surfaces plain meaning and hides raw JSON, machine ids, and source addresses.
Inline design-history comments with dates and regression references.
Private constants prefixed with underscore for internal thresholds and caches.
concerns: Hardcoded localhost reranker URL and timeout in _rerank_claims.
_THEOREM_CLAIMS_CACHE is in-process only and lost on restart.
Several functions use bare except/pass, swallowing errors silently.
prewarm_theorem_explains is documented as 'NOT WIRED at present'.
explanation_grounding is long and mixes retrieval, formatting, subtype policy, and caching.
Inline commentary is extensive and may drift from actual code behavior.
notes: This file is the recollection-side seam that assembles grounded, legible context for decision explanations, with structural safeguards to keep theorem-fork explanations tied to Tim's own mathematics.
questions: What is the exact schema of the decision dict consumed by explanation_grounding?
How do suite._embed_consult_query and suite.store._vector_records behave on failure?
What contract does recall_determine.determine provide for themes and claims?
How are board:// and code:// addresses normalized and validated elsewhere?
What triggers the vector version bump used for theorem claim cache invalidation?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches; the file is the explain-half memory retrieve seam and also builds the richer explanation_grounding bundle with theorem-fork safeguards and performance optimizations.
apparent_themes: Performance-aware retrieval (embed/snapshot once, skip wasted work)
intention_signals: Make decision explanations traceable to actual company memory and Tim's own framework, prevent AI-generated glosses from masquerading as Tim's theorem, and keep the fast card-resolve path within its 3-second budget while allowing a deeper explain turn.
novelty: A structural never-assert law for theorem-fork decisions implemented as a dedicated framework-only grounding path with chunk-traced claims and explicit AI-projection caveat, combined with live-calibrated rerank thresholds and aggressive avoidance of redundant embedding work.
contribution: A decision-shaped memory retrieval layer that bridges the shared corpus and the decision surface, producing grounded, legible context bundles and a specialized, traceable grounding path for theorem-fork decisions.
summary_for_embedding: Decision-memory retrieval module that queries the shared vector corpus across configured spaces, reranks results, resolves meaning and provenance, and assembles context bundles for decision explanations with theorem-fork safeguards.
intention_for_embedding: Provide the RHM with traceable, decision-specific grounding from company memory while protecting theorem-fork explanations from AI-projection.
suggested_fields: calibration_log — live-measured thresholds like _CTX_FLOOR with date and context
vector_version_invalidation_key — cache invalidation metadata for theorem claims
server_side_required_elements — registry mapping subtype element names to rendered texts

symbols.does: Pre-computes theorem-fork explanation grounding at startup to warm the claims cache.
symbols.description: Iterates the decision registry for subtype='theorem-fork' and runs explanation_grounding for each to populate _THEOREM_CLAIMS_CACHE, best-effort, returning the count warmed. Currently documented as not wired into startup.

symbols.does: Retrieves prior decision-shaped content about a topic from history and common_knowledge.
symbols.description: Frames the query as a prior-decision search, queries the two decision-bearing spaces, optionally reranks with jina-v3, and returns a list of source/space/score items representing decisions made about the topic before.

symbols.does: Converts a corpus record into a short, render-ready prose string.
symbols.description: Extracts the first meaningful string field from a record's output/text/content, handling structured dicts and lists by joining non-empty labeled fields, and truncates to max_chars.

symbols.does: Resolves an extraction:// address into a short meaning string.
symbols.description: Delegates to recall_determine.read_extraction and joins the about, summary, and top claims fields into a readable excerpt, returning empty on failure.

symbols.does: Attaches legible digest text to each context item in one pass.
symbols.description: Scans the corpus once to find the newest record per source address, reads each record, and uses _clean_meaning or _extraction_text to add a 'text' field so consumers never see raw ids.

symbols.does: Strips formatting and section-id markers from a provenance excerpt.
symbols.description: Removes markdown bold, code ticks, heading prefixes, bullets, and leading section markers while preserving the verbatim body content, producing operator-legible provenance text.

symbols.does: Resolves a decision's explanation_source into its decision-specific origin content.
symbols.description: Handles board:// (title+body) and code:// (whole file, JSON key path, or section-scoped anchor) sources, returning a cleaned excerpt or empty if the anchor/source is missing.

symbols.does: Builds the memory-context bundle for a pending decision.
symbols.description: Embeds once, snapshots vector records once, queries each configured space, optionally reranks the pooled hits with jina-v3, attaches digest text, adds neighbours and prior decisions, and returns a degrade-clean bundle with an honest note.

symbols.does: Reranks raw claim strings by jina-v3 relevance to the decision query.
symbols.description: Calls the localhost reranker directly with inline claim text, orders claims by relevance, and degrades to the original order on any error.

symbols.does: Assembles the authoritative explain-turn grounding bundle for a decision.
symbols.description: Chooses between fast decision-content/provenance grounding and full corpus recall, runs theorem-fork determine when needed, attaches the never-assert caveat, and renders a single operator-legible 'block' plus structured context, prior decisions, neighbours, theorem claims, and a grounding note.
